remarks: Leaves significantly eaten by leaf-rolling insects but this seems typical of the whole tree and other TIAM here
Leaves more or less full size but still with some spring color and not quite mature
Narrow crown with many other POTR5 around and underneath that are difficult to distinguish on imagery.
In a big canopy gap which allows it to be mostly sunlit
Whole canopy insect damaged; damage reflects that of entire TIAM population in the area.
This tree is probably barely visible from AOP and is in the understory. The sampled branch probably receives a few hours of direct light per day in the summer through a canopy gap to the south.
